mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in industrial production indicates expanding manufacturing output that drives corporate earnings growth, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 20-day returns are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) during months with rising INDPRO over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 8 percent, elevated labor market slack signals weak consumer demand and contracting corporate revenues, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 60-day returns are positive in more than 55% of instances when UNRATE > 8.0% over any 3-year rolling period.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month growth in retail sales and a declining unemployment rate confirm that demand expansion is matched by labor absorption rather than capacity constraints, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Jointly rising RSAFS and falling UNRATE fail to produce positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of realized months over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is pinned below 0.25 percent, an increase in housing starts indicates effective monetary policy transmission through the credit channel, lifting S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: During periods when FEDFUNDS < 0.25%, rising HOUST coincides with negative 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of triggered months over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter growth in real GDP signals broad-based aggregate demand expansion that lifts corporate revenue expectations and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive GDPC1 growth does not correlate with positive 60-day S&P 500 returns (Rank IC < 0.03) over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: An increase in nonfarm payrolls signals stronger labor income and consumption capacity, which market participants price rapidly into equities, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Months with rising PAYEMS do not produce significantly positive 5-day S&P 500 returns compared to months with falling PAYEMS over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index exceeds 220, elevated inflation expectations trigger tighter monetary policy, raising discount rates and compressing equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: When CPIAUCSL exceeds 220, S&P 500 forward 20-day returns are positive in more than 55% of triggered instances over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production alongside falling unemployment indicates a virtuous cycle of output growth and labor market health, which jointly amplify earnings expectations and drive positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Jointly rising INDPRO and falling UNRATE fail to produce positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of months over any 2-year rolling window.
